key_insight: Annealing temperature tightly controls phase selection in co-sputtered Fe-Mn-Sn films, with phase-pure Fe2MnSn emerging only in a narrow window around 580�b0C.
engineering_relevance: This identifies the critical thermal budget needed to fabricate ternary thin films with desirable magnetic, electrical, and magneto-optical properties for spintronic applications.
method: Co-sputter Fe, Mn, and Sn targets at room temperature near a 2:1:1 composition.
Anneal films from 400 to 700�b0C and compare phase evolution.
Correlate structural changes with electrical, magnetic, and magneto-optical responses.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a clear phase transition sequence: Fe3Sn2, Fe5Sn3, and Fe appear at 400�b0C-550�b0C, Fe2MnSn dominates at 580�b0C, and Mn becomes dominant by 750�b0C.
watch_item: Fe2MnSn appears highly sensitive to annealing temperature and shows poor thickness scaling below 100 nm, which may complicate reproducible device fabrication.
paper_title: Effect of annealing temperature on the structure and properties of co-sputtered Fe-Mn-Sn films near 2:1:1 ratio
